rulings.Case Title: LUIS E. MORELL VS. VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Volkswagen Group Of America, Inc.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 29, 2026, line 1. DEFENDANT VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.'S Motion To Tax Costs. The motion to tax costs is granted in part. The court strikes $3445 from the cost bill in expert witness fees and otherwise denies the motion to tax costs. The total amount of costs awarded is $6,665.50. Ordinarily, expert witness fees are not recoverable as costs unless ordered by the court. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 1033.5, subd. (a)(8).) Where a party serves an offer to compromise pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, section 998, this code provides in subdivision (d) as follows: "If an offer made by a plaintiff is not accepted and the defendant fails to obtain a more favorable judgment or award in any action or proceeding other than an eminent domain action, the court or arbitrator, in its discretion, may require the defendant to pay a reasonable sum to cover postoffer costs of the services of expert witnesses . . . ." Here, Plaintiff has not attached the 998 offer and no judgment has entered. Rather, the notice of settlement anticipates a dismissal by plaintiff, which is treated as a favorable judgment for defendant unless the parties agree otherwise. (The court assumes that the parties' settlement provided that Plaintiff could claim costs; Defendant makes no argument that costs cannot be awarded at all.) The court denies the motion as to the deposition fees sought by Plaintiff. The costs are recoverable on their face and Defendant has not demonstrated them to be unnecessary. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: TERI KOSCINSKI ET AL VS. PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 29, 2026, line 2. DEFENDANT TOWN OF DANVILLE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues. Defendant the Town of Danville's motion for summary judgment is denied. Danville's motion for summary judgment is denied. Its motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the negligent supervision and hiring cause of action and is denied as to all other causes of action. The request for judicial notice is granted. rulings.Case Title: MARYANN VELACRUZ VS. GRM INFORMATION MANAGEMENT SERVICES OF CALIFORNIA, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Plaintiff Maryann Velacruz To Submit To Second Medical Examination; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Aron K. Liang
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 29, 2026, line 3. DEFENDANT GRM INFORMATION MANAGEMENT SERVICES OF CALIFORNIA, LLC, GRM INFORMATION MANAGEMENT SERVICES OF SAN, OUSSEMA GHAZOUANI'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff Maryann Velacruz To Submit To Second Medical Examination. Defendant's motion to compel plaintiff Maryann Velacruz to submit to a second medical examination by neurologist Marilyn Robertson, M.D., is granted. Defendant demonstrates good cause: Velacruz seeks damages for two types of injury that are commonly diagnosed and treated by doctors of different specialties. The court orders the parties to meet and confer about the examination date; the examination shall occur within 30 days unless both parties agree otherwise. The place, manner, scope, and nature of examination shall be as set out in defendant's demand for physical examination set out in Exhibit 5 to the Liang Declaration. Defendant seeks sanctions. The court finds that plaintiff acted with substantial justification and does not award sanctions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: HBL HOLDINGS, LLC, VS. FELLOW INDUSTRIES, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Select Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 29, 2026, line 6. PLAINTIFF HBL HOLDINGS, LLC'S Motion To Seal Select Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint. Continued on the court's own motion to February 24, 2026. Plaintiff's unopposed motion to seal is supported by an attorney declaration reciting facts on information and belief. This is an insufficient evidentiary showing for factual findings. A motion to seal pursuant to Rule 2.550 requires the court to make factual findings. Plaintiff may supplement with a proper declaration. In addition, Plaintiff is directed to supply courtesy copies of its moving papers to Department 301 at least five court days in advance of the hearing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing.
